rows.notes: Top-of-page title. Letters are fairly legible but orthography is archaic/uncertain (looks like 'Arrearija Anni 1701'). No amounts present and central heading style marks this as a header.
Amount columns clearly aligned; reads 60 0 0. The description handwriting is partially indistinct (first word begins with G/Q and second word uncertain) so I transcribed literally as it appears.
Description and all three amount columns are clear and well-aligned. Pounds 26, shillings 8, pence 0 are unambiguous.

Amounts read in the three narrow right-hand columns as '70 / 8 / 8' but the description is hard to decipher (letters uncertain). Column alignment seems correct; chosen digits match visible loops but some ink blots make letters ambiguous.

Amounts (30 0 0) are clear and well aligned. The description is mostly legible as 'South-... pro Mick' though one internal word/letter is slightly abbreviated; chosen transcription follows the visible letters.
Clear numeric columns: reads 21 pounds, 19 shillings, 4 pence. Description script is legible as 'Merton pro Biernio'.
Amounts 2/17/9 are clearly positioned in the three columns. The name 'Chace-Hill pro Mick' is readily readable.
Amount columns read 2 / 7 / 1 clearly. The place-name is abbreviated and reads 'Thrup' (the middle letters slightly indistinct), hence slightly reduced confidence on description.

Amounts (2/15/0) clear in the columns. The description appears to say 'Quit Rent' (preceded by a short word like 'Gae' or 'Gae'), letters for the first word are not fully clear, so transcription follows the visible form.
Very clear handwriting for description and amounts; reads 5/0/0.
Description 'Ralph Marshalls Capons' is legible; amount columns read 0/3/0 without ambiguity.

Amount columns align to 1 / 7 / 0 but the pence column has a small trailing mark that might be a fractional symbol or stray ink; I interpreted as 0 pence and no fraction. Description letters are slightly cramped making the first word uncertain.
Clear: 'Addington Mills pro Mick' and amounts 4/6/11 are well formed and in-range (pence 11 valid).
Description and amounts (2/0/0) are clearly legible.

Amounts (2/5/0) are clear, but the descriptive phrase includes an abbreviated Latin/technical phrase (appears like 'Quadriennio' or similar) that is partially faded/abbreviated — transcription follows visible letters with some uncertainty.
Clear reading: name 'Hughs' and amounts 0/16/0 clearly placed and within valid ranges.
Amounts 0/10/0 are clear and description 'Greenway pro Mick' is legible.
Well-aligned amounts 0/8/6 and clear name 'Walker' make this high confidence.
Amounts 0/11/8 clearly visible; description reads 'Minne pro Mick' with legible handwriting.
Amount 0/8/0 is readable, but the description is crossed/skewed and partially overwritten by a slight blot; I read the place as 'Cauder Gate' though letters are ambiguous.
Numeric columns show '2 / 3 / 5' but there are stray pen-strokes near the pence column (small extra marks) — I interpreted main pence digit as 5. Description 'Brome' is readable but slightly slanted.
Columns align to 2 / 0 / 5, though there are faint additional marks after the pence that look like pen-smears; description likely 'Saler' or 'Sader'—letters are not crisp.

This is a multi-name/bracketed line (several names written on one line). Numbers read roughly as 4 / 14 / 8 but there are extra small digits or marginal notes nearby (e.g. '89' or '9') that make the exact punctuation/annotation uncertain. Handwriting here is crowded and interlined; amount chosen from the three aligned columns.
Amount columns read 4/10/8 clearly. Description looks like 'Clifton ferry' though the middle word could be 'ferry' or 'ferrye'—transcribed as appears.
Numbers 1/13/8 are legible and in valid ranges. Description 'Benjington' reads clearly though spelling could vary historically.
Amounts 7/14/4 are evident. The place-name is somewhat stylized script but reads as 'Braythorne' (letters clear enough to be confident).

Numbers 3/2/0 are clear. The descriptive phrase is long and abbreviated (appears as 'South & Newton Fermeed pro Bienniox' or similar); letters are partially faded/abbreviated so transcription attempts to preserve visible characters but remains uncertain.
Large sum in columns reads 51/17/0 clearly. The description reads 'Tickhamn before the lease' (handwriting for 'before the lease' is slightly compressed but legible).

rows.description: Tickhamn since the Lease pro sequin
rows.notes: Amounts 9/0/0 are clear. The description 'since the lease pro ...' has abbreviations and a marginal abbreviation that is not fully clear (transcribed as 'sequin' to reflect visible letters).
Amounts read 0/16/0 clearly. 'Burton Vicarage' is legible; final Latin/abbreviated suffix is less certain but follows visible letters.
Final line shows a bracketed pair of names with '8s' each; the total amount in columns is clearly 16/0/0. Parenthetical names and '8s' abbreviations are transcribed as they appear.
